subjectIncludes: Lock God Mode extractor system contract
Add newsletter and creator source-stack contract

systemArea: Foundation Build Intel / God Mode extractor system
acceptanceState: Verified contract/preflight stab accepted; extractor implementation is not done and live extraction approval is still pending.
whatChanged: Promoted the full God Mode extractor requirement from a parked umbrella idea into a P0 parent runtime and source SOP contract, rewrote YouTube catch-up so it cannot mean video-only watching, and expanded the contract to creator source stacks, free newsletters, free Skool/community, and safe free resources/downloads.
whatItDoes: Defines the reusable extractor layers and source-specific SOPs: source packet, metadata triage, Read, Ears, Eyes, Hands, Brain, Evidence, Boundaries, Output, Grading, and Autopilot. The source stack SOP requires every approved creator to show YouTube, blog/site, newsletter, GitHub/docs/resources, free Skool/community, paid Skool/course/training platforms, and other source surfaces. MyICOR is one paid course/training platform instance, not its own top-level source family or tag. The YouTube SOP covers metadata triage, latest-10 creator baseline, full video/audio/visual watch, whole page/description extraction, resource-link packets, public/free auto-read follow-up, free newsletter intake, safe free-resource capture, free Skool/community handling, paid-gate value evaluation, creator grade update, and morning autopilot reporting.
whyItMatters: Steve has repeatedly corrected that God Mode extraction means taking the value around the source, not just watching videos. Without this parent contract, future builders can keep spending budget on partial extraction and call it done.
whereItLives: docs/process/extractor-eyes-hands-brain-runtime-001-plan.md parent runtime and source SOP plan
lib/god-mode-extractor-system-contract.js contract snapshot, source SOPs, backlog update text, and dogfood proof
scripts/process-god-mode-extractor-system-contract-check.mjs focused no-spend proof and guarded live backlog stab
lib/foundation-backlog-seed-chunks/chunk-005.js parent and YouTube card seed truth
lib/dev-intel-source-coverage.js Dev source-family rows for creator source stack, newsletters, and free Skool communities
lib/god-mode-extractor-parity-gate.js source-family parity row for creator newsletters
lib/source-family-god-mode-extractors.js source-family maturity boundary for creator newsletters
public/dev.js planned source cards for Creator Source Stack, Creator Newsletters, and Skool / Free Communities
docs/rebuild/current-plan.md active blocker and build order truth
docs/rebuild/current-state.md active blocker and current-state truth
live backlog_items rows for EXTRACTOR-EYES-HANDS-BRAIN-RUNTIME-001 and YOUTUBE-CREATOR-GOD-MODE-CATCHUP-001
proofStatus: Focused proof validates the parent card is live P0 scoped and not parked, YouTube catch-up blocks video-only God Mode completion, the parent plan preserves Steve requirement details, source SOPs cover creator source stacks, YouTube, newsletters, Skool free, Skool paid/private, paid course/training platforms, and GitHub/docs/public resources, and dogfood rejects video-only, missing Hands, and unsafe extraction/provider/external-write variants. Source coverage, source-family maturity, parity, and Dev Hub proofs expose newsletters/free communities as planned lanes without false God Mode claims and keep MyICOR as an instance of paid course/training platforms, not its own tag. This is a preflight contract closeout, not accepted implementation completion; broad live extraction remains approval-bound and pending. Backlog hygiene is healthy, YouTube readback is healthy but baseline-incomplete, and foundation:verify passes 519/519.
reviewNext: Implement the YouTube SOP runner/read model end to end before more broad live YouTube spend: all creator rows visible, metadata triage, latest-10 baseline, full watch, whole page/description extraction, resource packets, newsletter intake, creator source-stack readback, free-community runner proof, safe free-resource capture, paid-gate evaluation, creator grade update, and morning autopilot. Do not mark the parent extractor done until that implementation proof passes.
knownLimits: This is the requirement/contract stab, not the full extractor implementation.

This does not run YouTube extraction, call Gemini, spend provider budget, crawl live pages, log in, buy, download, sign up for newsletters, join communities, submit forms, post, comment, message, mutate credentials, write externally, or auto-create backlog cards.
This does not approve Skool, MyICOR, paid/private/member/course extraction. Free community/newsletter lanes are policy-approved but still need runtime implementation/proof before autonomous live use.
This does not close YOUTUBE-CREATOR-GOD-MODE-CATCHUP-001 or claim all approved creators are fully God Mode complete.

whatChanged: Closed the V1 Build Intel source pipeline truth for public YouTube selection, bounded full-watch readback, resource-link disposition, Dev source slicing, source-family coverage, and source-value grading.
whatItDoes: Provides the proven no-surprise pipeline between approved public YouTube/internal source evidence and Dev recommendations: select bounded public videos, verify persisted Gemini full-watch batches, classify resource links, route Dev-relevant shared-source signals, show source-family coverage, and grade source value by lane.
whyItMatters: The Dev system can now compare source-backed Build Intel instead of relying on transcript-only snippets, manual link chasing, or whichever creator happened to be processed first. Broader live extraction and build promotion still stay behind separate approval gates.
whereItLives: lib/youtube-latest-20-intel-run.js public manifest selector and long-course risk routing
lib/youtube-latest-20-full-watch-runner.js guarded Gemini API full-watch runner and persisted report readback
lib/youtube-resource-link-resolver.js safe public link resolver and blocked-link dispositions
proofCommands: node --check lib/youtube-latest-20-intel-run.js lib/youtube-latest-20-full-watch-runner.js lib/youtube-resource-link-resolver.js lib/dev-source-slice-router.js lib/dev-intel-source-coverage.js lib/build-intel-source-value-grader.js
proofStatus: Focused proofs validate a 9-video public/no-auth manifest, persisted full-watch batch readback, 60 live resource links with exact blockers, 40 Dev shared-source candidates with 40 ops-only items parked, 9 Dev source families with blocked Skool/MyICOR visibility, 26 live source grades by lane, no surprise live fetch, no new live Gemini spend, no auto backlog promotion, no auth/private crawling, no source-system mutation, and no external writes.
reviewNext: Continue `YOUTUBE-CREATOR-GOD-MODE-CATCHUP-001` through scheduler dry-run review or a fresh Steve-approved live-bounded budget. After catch-up/source parity is visible, use `DEV-BUILD-OPPORTUNITY-SCOPER-001`, `BUILD-PORTFOLIO-SCRUM-MASTER-001`, and `BUILD-OPPORTUNITY-PROMOTION-GATE-001` before any build work is promoted.
knownLimits: This closes the V1 contracts/tools, not total public YouTube creator catch-up.
This does not run new live Gemini extraction, spend provider budget, or enable unattended scheduler execution.
This does not crawl Skool, MyICOR, paid/private/member/course/community/comment sources.
This does not submit forms, log in, buy, download, post, comment, message, mutate credentials, or write externally.
This does not auto-create backlog cards, approve build promotion, or let source grades delete/watchlist-kill sources without review.
Long-course public videos remain routed out of the standard full-watch lane until a separate bounded long-course runner exists.

subjectIncludes: Promote God Mode Hands reset truth

whatChanged: Closed the public YouTube comments card as an operator-exclusion decision instead of an active or parked extractor lane.
whatItDoes: Keeps historical comment-capture proof as boundary evidence only while preventing comments from being treated as a God Mode blocker, next sprint item, API-key setup task, runner integration task, Director input, or LLM/runtime spend target.
whyItMatters: Steve explicitly rejected comment reading as low-value noise for this sprint. The actual God Mode gap is browser Hands plus approved source-packet worker execution.
proofStatus: Focused proof now guards the exclusion instead of running a comment adapter: comments are operator-excluded, not active work, not parked future work, and not a God Mode blocker.
reviewNext: Continue `EXTRACTOR-HANDS-BROWSER-RUNTIME-001` and `SOURCE-PACKET-WORKER-RUNNER-001`; do not revisit comments unless Steve explicitly reverses the exclusion.
knownLimits: Historical comment extraction code remains as boundary evidence but should not be scheduled or integrated.
This does not delete old proof artifacts.
This does not build browser Hands or source-packet worker persistence.

whatChanged: Closed the God Mode extractor parity gate as the source-family truth layer: 13 source families now have capability status, blockers, next cards, false-claim rejection, and operator-visible Dev Data Pool rendering.
whatItDoes: Prevents transcript-only or partial lanes from being labeled full God Mode. The focused proof validates the source-family matrix, dogfoods false God Mode claims for partial YouTube and paid Skool, keeps YouTube comments operator-excluded, and renders the parity matrix from the Foundation payload on `/dev`.
whyItMatters: Steve corrected the mission: extractors need eyes, ears, hands, reading, brain, evidence, boundaries, and output before the system uses them as trusted intake. This gate keeps future builder work honest while source-family hardening continues.
whereItLives: scripts/process-dev-team-hub-v0-check.mjs Dev UI parity section regression proof
public/dev.html God Mode parity panel mount
proofStatus: Focused proof covers 13 source families, confirms the current system has zero full-God-Mode claims, dogfoods false claims for partial YouTube and paid Skool as failures, validates truthful Dev wording, and checks that `/dev` renders the parity matrix from the Foundation payload.
reviewNext: Continue `YOUTUBE-CREATOR-GOD-MODE-CATCHUP-001`, `SOURCE-FAMILY-GOD-MODE-EXTRACTORS-001`, and `SYNTHESIS-ROUTER-FRESHNESS-TRIGGER-001`. Use this matrix to select source-family hardening and do not claim full God Mode until the gate says the source-specific capability set is proven.
knownLimits: This does not claim any source family is full God Mode today.
This does not run extraction, crawl links, approve source packets, log in, buy, download, submit forms, post, comment, message, or write externally.
This does not approve Skool, MyICOR, paid/private/member/course/community/session work.
This does not close source-family hardening or creator catch-up.
YouTube comments remain operator-excluded unless Steve explicitly reverses that decision.

systemArea: Foundation source freshness and synthesis routing
whatChanged: Closed the synthesis freshness trigger so source/archive/extractor activity is compared against synthesis and Action Router freshness through the Foundation job ledger.
whatItDoes: Completed Foundation jobs now patch synthesisFreshness metadata, and the focused snapshot reports stale, blocked-by-extractor, waiting-for-extractor, action-router-due, or fresh. Follow-up synthesis/action-router jobs remain no-spend and explicit unless SYNTHESIS_FRESHNESS_TRIGGER_AUTORUN=true is set.
whyItMatters: Fresh source extraction is not valuable if the brain layer quietly stays stale. This prevents Gmail, Missive, YouTube, Slack, meetings, or other source-family work from looking synthesized when extractors or synthesis have not caught up.
whereItLives: lib/synthesis-router-freshness-trigger.js freshness snapshot, classifier, metadata, and hook policy
scripts/run-foundation-job.mjs runtime hook after job completion
proofStatus: Focused proof validates stale, failed-extractor, post-synthesis, post-action-router, runtime hook, live metadata patching, no-spend autorun gating, package script, plan proof cases, and no destination writes. Live ledger read reports waiting_for_extractor instead of fake freshness.
reviewNext: Repair waiting/blocked source-family extractor jobs, then decide whether to enable bounded synthesis/action-router autorun. Keep destination writes human-approved.
knownLimits: This does not repair Gmail/Missive/Slack/meeting extractor failures.
This does not enable autorun by default.
This does not auto-write backlog, decisions, tasks, source systems, external systems, or Git reports.
This does not make any source family full God Mode.

whyItMatters: The system can now say exactly which source families are partial, blocked, waiting for extractor repair, operator-excluded, planned, or mature without pretending transcript-only, public-only, or paid/private parked sources are full God Mode.
whereItLives: lib/source-family-god-mode-extractors.js maturity snapshot, evaluator, and dogfood proof

subjectIncludes: Add source packet worker runner persistence

systemArea: Foundation Build Intel source-packet worker execution
whatChanged: Added the approved source-packet worker runner persistence/status layer on top of the exact public-page runtime.
whatItDoes: Approved public/free/GitHub/sales-page source-packet decisions can now be validated, run through the exact-page worker, persisted as source-crawl status rows and intelligence report proof artifacts, surfaced as ready/already-run/blocked queue rows, and returned to Dev approval review as worker readiness without starting from approval.
whyItMatters: Approval records alone do not extract source evidence. This closes the public source-packet decision -> worker -> artifact -> freshness/status loop without broad crawling, login, forms, downloads, purchases, external writes, or auto backlog mutation.
whereItLives: lib/source-packet-worker-runner.js runner validation, artifact builder, persistence, queue read model, and dogfood proof
lib/source-packet-public-web-runtime.js exact public-page runtime reused by the runner
lib/foundation-build-intel-routes.js guarded worker run and queue routes
public/dev.js worker-readiness status after approval decisions

proofStatus: Focused proof dogfoods approved packet execution, source-crawl/report-artifact persistence through injected stores, decision-ledger queue status before/after completion, held/rejected/Skool/follow-link/live-without-allow blocks, and no external/backlog/link-following side effects.
reviewNext: Continue `EXTRACTOR-HANDS-BROWSER-RUNTIME-001` so source-appropriate browser Hands can click and navigate inside approved source-packet boundaries. Keep Skool/MyICOR/private/auth work blocked until Steve approves source-specific session/content boundaries.
knownLimits: This is exact URL only and does not follow discovered links; discovered links become new source-packet candidates.
The Dev approval action still does not start the worker.
Live exact-URL mode requires explicit allowLive=true and remains separate from the no-network synthetic proof.

No source-specific logged-in Hands, Skool/MyICOR course/community crawling, form submission, download, purchase, post, comment, message, credential mutation, external write, or auto backlog write is included.

whatChanged: Closed the public YouTube autonomous watch scheduler V1 as a guarded dry-run/live-bounded scheduler that selects exact public videos from live Foundation truth under source-grade, budget, duplicate, long-course, and approval rules.
whatItDoes: Builds a next-watch plan from the daily watch manifest, source-value grader, and same-day Gemini spend; refuses unsafe/private/paid/long-course/comment sources; can call the existing exact-video full-watch runner only when live-bounded budget approval is explicit; then refreshes Director, source grader, and Dev Hub after a successful live run.
whyItMatters: The YouTube catch-up mission should not require Steve or Codex to hand-pick every public video forever, but the scheduler must still stop on budget, provider, source-boundary, and approval risks.
whereItLives: lib/youtube-god-mode-autonomous-watch-scheduler.js selection, budget, retry, runner-command, and report logic

proofStatus: Focused proof validates source-grade selection, ungraded exploratory sampling, C/D throttling, duplicate/private/long-course rejection, run/day budget stops, provider retry caps, live approval requirements, exact-video guarded full-watch runner command, post-run refresh commands, disabled/manual job row, live dry-run manifest/source-grade readback, and no backlog or external writes. Live dry-run currently selects 3 videos with about $0.7894 remaining under the $15 daily cap.
reviewNext: Continue `YOUTUBE-CREATOR-GOD-MODE-CATCHUP-001`: use the scheduler dry-run to review next videos, then run only with a fresh Steve-approved live-bounded budget or a new daily cap.
knownLimits: The scheduled Foundation job row remains disabled/manual; this does not enable unattended live Gemini spending.

The scheduler dogfood proves a source-SOP-incomplete row with a new public video is selected, while a claim-only SOP row without a new video remains rejected.

whatChanged: Added a governed browser Hands V1 runtime for approved public/free source packets and promoted its truth into the God Mode extractor parity gate.
whatItDoes: A packet can now be classified as exact public read ready, click/navigation ready, or blocked for missing detail, auth/session, paid/private, purchase/form, or unsupported source family. The runtime can capture one approved synthetic click/navigation evidence artifact with selector, href, final URL, page hashes, headings, text preview, stop reason, and side-effect proof while local Playwright live mode remains explicit allowLive only.
whyItMatters: God Mode extractors cannot be real if they are page readers that cannot click. This closes the first source-packet-bounded Hands contract without pretending Skool/MyICOR/private/auth source families are solved.
whereItLives: lib/extractor-hands-browser-runtime.js Hands policy, status classifier, validator, artifact builder, synthetic runner, and local Playwright live mode
proofStatus: Focused proof dogfoods one approved public/free click/navigation artifact and blocks missing packet detail, broad allowed-next patterns, auth sessions, form selectors, downloads, purchase/checkout links, Skool/auth sources, external writes, and backlog writes. The proof launches no live browser and performs no network fetch.
reviewNext: Continue `EXTRACTOR-HANDS-PRODUCTION-RUNNER-001` to wire this bounded Hands runtime into approved source-packet worker queue/run UI and production-safe artifact persistence. Keep Skool/MyICOR/private/auth Hands blocked until Steve approves source-specific session/content boundaries.
knownLimits: This V1 proof is synthetic/no-network and does not run a production public webpage by default.
Live Playwright mode exists but requires explicit allowLive=true and an approved exact packet.
This does not build the Dev run button/status UI for Hands queue rows.

This does not approve Skool, MyICOR, paid/private/member/course, login/session, form submission, download, purchase, post, comment, message, credential mutation, external write, or auto backlog write behavior.

subjectIncludes: Add source packet approval decision ledger

whatChanged: Added a durable source-packet approval decision ledger for Dev link review and updated God Mode parity truth to show public/resource links have decision persistence but not an auto-starting worker.
whatItDoes: Dev link review can now record Steve approve, hold, or reject decisions as source-crawl decision records with exact packet boundaries. The record survives reloads, explains what happened in plain English, and remains separate from any crawler or source worker.
whyItMatters: Steve should be able to approve or reject YouTube resource links without manually chasing them and without accidentally starting a crawl, login, purchase, form submission, backlog write, credential mutation, or external write.
whereItLives: lib/source-packet-approval-decision-ledger.js decision record builder, validation, fake persistence, and dogfood proof
lib/foundation-build-intel-routes.js Dev Hub source-packet decision route
server.js injected source-crawl target/item persistence dependencies
lib/source-lifecycle.js source lifecycle target/budget baseline alignment
lib/build-intel-watchlist.js Brad Bonanno /watch video source ref
proofCommands: node --check lib/source-packet-approval-decision-ledger.js scripts/process-source-packet-approval-decision-ledger-check.mjs lib/foundation-build-intel-routes.js public/dev.js server.js lib/build-intel-watchlist.js lib/god-mode-extractor-parity-gate.js lib/foundation-verify-coverage-card-ids.js lib/source-lifecycle.js lib/foundation-build-closeout-source-records.js
proofStatus: Focused proof dogfoods approve, hold, reject, unsafe runtime tampering, and manual-packet approval failure; Dev Hub proof validates the only write surface is the source-packet decision ledger; parity proof keeps full God Mode claims false; backlog hygiene was healthy with 843 cards and 0 findings before ship.
reviewNext: Source-packet worker and Hands production runner are now closed. Continue source-specific Skool/MyICOR/private/auth packet/session cards before any private or paid source run.
knownLimits: Recording approval does not start a crawler, source worker, login, purchase, form submission, external write, backlog write, credential mutation, or browser-profile mutation.
Manual/ambiguous source packets still fail closed and need an adjusted packet before approval.
Paid/private/community/course/auth sources remain blocked until source-specific approval and worker boundaries are proven.

The Brad Bonanno /watch video is extracted as source evidence, but its ideas are not implementation-ready until Director/Scoper review compares them against BCrew's existing Gemini route, source-packet approvals, and hands/navigation gap.

subjectIncludes: Expose Dev Hub Foundation Done bar

whatChanged: Added a compact read-only Foundation Done bar to Dev Hub that projects source-maturity truth into extracted, atomized, synthesized, routed, and resolved/applied stages.
whatItDoes: The Dev Hub now shows per-source pipeline progress from extraction through applied action-route resolution. Routed-but-unapplied sources remain open gaps, and waiting route pressure stays visible instead of being hidden behind a routed count.
whyItMatters: Steve is using Dev Hub as the template for the rest of AIOS. This makes the first template show whether intelligence is flowing end to end or sitting still, before any new automation or live route apply work.
whereItLives: lib/dev-hub-foundation-done-bar.js compact source-maturity projection, validation, and dogfood proof
public/dev-foundation-done-bar.js standalone renderer on Dev Hub snapshot events
scripts/process-dev-hub-foundation-done-bar-check.mjs focused proof and guarded close-card path
proofStatus: Focused proof dogfoods the exact failure mode: a routed-but-unapplied source remains a resolved-stage gap and cannot count as Foundation done. Live readback validates 43 source contracts, 30 extracted, 2 atomized, 30 synthesized, 21 routed, 1 resolved/applied, and 103 waiting routes, with bounded payload rows and no route, destination, backlog, Harlan, extraction, model, or external write path.
reviewNext: Use the Foundation Done bar to choose the next read-only repair slice: either Scoper readback from Director recommendations or a source-family atom-flow repair. Do not approve/apply live routes or auto-promote build cards without Steve confirming the exact route/card.
knownLimits: This is a read-only visibility slice; it does not repair stale atom flow, run synthesis, run extraction, approve/apply routes, create recommendations, send Harlan, call a model, or write externally.
The bar intentionally requires an applied action-route signal before a source counts as resolved; route-only or pending-review work remains open.
The Dev Hub surface is still above the 3,000-line caution threshold in existing files, so future work should keep adding standalone modules/renderers or split files before broad UI changes.

subjectIncludes: Expose Dev Hub Scoper evidence trace readback

systemArea: Dev Hub Scoper evidence trace visibility
whatChanged: Added a compact read-only Scoper evidence trace readback to Dev Hub that projects existing Director-to-Scoper trace proof into reviewed, raw-trace-ready, Portfolio-ready, parked, and proposal-only states.
whatItDoes: The Dev Hub now shows which current Director recommendations have raw atom and raw hit proof before Scoper/Portfolio review, and which recommendations stay parked because source evidence is missing or blocked.
whyItMatters: Steve wants Dev Hub to recommend next builds from real evidence without auto-building or hiding promotion gaps. This makes Scoper readiness visible while keeping recommendations proposal-only.
whereItLives: lib/dev-hub-scoper-evidence-trace-readback.js compact trace projection, validation, and dogfood proof
lib/foundation-build-intel-routes.js existing Scoper evidence trace readback for Dev Hub API
public/dev-scoper-evidence-trace.js standalone renderer on Dev Hub snapshot events
public/dev-scoper-evidence-trace.css standalone Scoper Trace panel styles
scripts/process-dev-hub-scoper-evidence-trace-readback-check.mjs focused proof and guarded close-card path
proofStatus: Focused proof dogfoods the exact failure modes: a candidate without raw atom and raw hit proof cannot be ready for Portfolio review, and any auto-promoted candidate fails validation. Live readback validates current Director candidates through existing Scoper evidence trace with bounded rows and no backlog, route, Harlan, extraction, model, or external write path.
reviewNext: Use Foundation Done plus Scoper trace to choose the next safe repair slice: source-family atom-flow repair/readback or Dev Hub self-cleaning lifecycle readback. Do not create live build cards, approve/apply routes, or auto-promote recommendations without Steve confirming the exact card.
knownLimits: This is a read-only visibility slice; it does not create Scoper cards, Portfolio records, backlog cards, Current Sprint overlays, action routes, Harlan messages, extraction runs, model calls, or external writes.
Portfolio-ready means raw trace is available for review; it is not Steve approval to build.
The fifth current Director candidate is parked when raw source evidence is incomplete; this card surfaces that boundary but does not repair the source atom gap.

subjectIncludes: Expose Dev Hub intelligence hygiene readback

whatChanged: Added a compact read-only Intelligence Hygiene readback to Dev Hub that ranks source pipeline gaps, action-route cleanup pressure, Scoper parked recommendations, and source-family boundaries from existing read models.
whatItDoes: The Dev Hub now shows what to clean, park, or review next without writing atoms, facts, chunks, routes, backlog cards, Scoper records, Portfolio records, Harlan messages, extraction runs, model calls, or external side effects.
whyItMatters: Steve wants the Dev Hub loop to keep the intelligence pipe clean while new data arrives, but false freshness and auto-promotion would recreate the old built-but-untrusted pattern. This surfaces the cleanup queue and keeps repair work proposal-only until the exact source evidence and approval boundary are clear.
whereItLives: lib/dev-hub-intelligence-hygiene-readback.js compact hygiene projection, validation, and dogfood proof
lib/dev-team-hub.js intelligenceHygieneReadback payload wiring from existing read models
public/dev-intelligence-hygiene.js standalone renderer on Dev Hub snapshot events
public/dev-intelligence-hygiene.css standalone Intelligence Hygiene panel styles
scripts/process-dev-hub-intelligence-hygiene-readback-check.mjs focused proof and guarded close-card path
proofStatus: Focused proof dogfoods the exact failure modes: atom-flow gaps must carry a false-freshness warning, and any auto-mutation fails validation. Live readback validates current cleanup pressure with bounded queues and no atom, fact, chunk, route, destination, backlog, Scoper, Portfolio, approval, Harlan, extraction, model, or external write path.
reviewNext: Use the Intelligence Hygiene buckets to choose the next specific safe repair. Do not write fresh atoms from stale facts, approve/apply live routes, or promote recommendations into Scoper/backlog without exact source-backed evidence and Steve approval.
knownLimits: This is a read-only visibility slice; it does not repair stale atom flow, run extraction, run synthesis, approve/apply routes, create build cards, send Harlan, call a model, or write externally.
The panel ranks cleanup pressure from existing read models; it is not a Steve approval or a Scoper promotion.
Future repairs must choose one bucket and dogfood the exact failure mode with fresh source-backed proof.
